title: Universal App Manifest (UAM) v7.0
description: A universal, framework-agnostic standard for describing a reactive web application. UAM v7 adds: store parity with components (watch, destroy, subscribe, lifecycle), non-reactive instance refs, deep reactivity hints per target, integration timing hints (deferInit, scanAfterRender), and element-level CSS transitions. All v7 additions are optional — existing v2-v6 manifests remain valid.

entry.description: The root component name to render

scripts.description: External script URLs or paths to include (e.g., Chart.js, plugins). Note: Scripts for declared integrations are auto-included.

inlineScripts.description: Inline JavaScript code blocks to include. Prefer 'helpers' for pure functions and 'integrations' for library setup.

styles.description: Global CSS styles for the application

helpers.description: Pure utility functions available in expressions and templates. These are framework-agnostic and compiled to appropriate patterns for each target.

integrations.description: Third-party library configurations. Compilers generate framework-appropriate initialization, data binding, and cleanup code.

plugins.description: UAM plugins to include. Plugins are resolved from /www/js/uam/plugins/

stores.description: Global reactive stores (like Pinia, Redux, or WF stores)

variables.description: Documentation of compile-time variables used in this manifest. Use {{variableName|"defaultValue"}} syntax anywhere in the manifest.

default.description: Default value (should match the default in placeholders)

examples.description: Example values this variable might take

targets.description: Per-target framework configuration for AI-assisted code generation. Links idiom guides, external references, and generation hints for each compilation target.

idioms.description: Relative path to the framework idiom guide (e.g., './idioms/react.idioms.md')

references.description: URLs to framework documentation, llms.txt files, or other references for AI assistants

hints.description: Optional hints for AI code generation for this target.

deepReactivity.description: Whether the target supports deep reactive proxies. true: direct nested mutations auto-propagate. false: use immutable patterns (spread, produce).

helpersDefinition.description: Pure utility functions that can be called from expressions and templates. Functions must be pure (no side effects) and framework-agnostic.

oneOf.description: Function body as code string. Parameters are inferred or use arrow function syntax: '(hex) => { return hex.slice(1); }'

pure.description: Whether this function is pure (no side effects). Non-pure helpers may not be optimizable.

examples.getLuminance: (hex) => { const r = parseInt(hex.slice(1,3), 16) / 255; const g = parseInt(hex.slice(3,5), 16) / 255; const b = parseInt(hex.slice(5,7), 16) / 255; return 0.2126 * r + 0.7152 * g + 0.0722 * b; }

getTextColor.description: Returns appropriate text color (dark or light) for a given background color

integrationsDefinition.description: Third-party library configurations. Each integration type has its own schema. Compilers auto-generate init, update, and destroy code appropriate for each target framework.

chartjsIntegration.description: Chart.js visualization configuration. Compilers generate framework-appropriate canvas access, chart initialization, reactive updates, and cleanup.

cdn.description: CDN URL for Chart.js (auto-included if not in scripts)

component.description: Component name where this chart lives. If omitted, applies to any component with matching target.

target.description: CSS selector for the canvas element within the component (e.g., 'canvas', '.chart-canvas', '#myChart')

data.description: State path for chart data array (e.g., 'state.revenueHistory', 'store:sales.revenueHistory')

datasetLabel.description: Label for the dataset (shown in legend)

options.description: Chart.js options object (passed directly to Chart constructor)

updateMode.description: Chart.js update mode for reactive updates

deferInit.description: Initialization timing. 'afterPaint': defer to requestAnimationFrame (library needs rendered dimensions). 'afterMount': defer to next microtask (DOM must settle). 'immediate': init synchronously.

scanAfterRender.description: This integration creates DOM that may contain framework components. Frameworks requiring explicit scanning (WildflowerJS) should scan after render.

datatablesIntegration.description: DataTables table configuration. Compilers generate framework-appropriate table initialization, reactive data binding, and cleanup. For WildflowerJS, auto-generates wildflower.scan() on draw events when columns contain components.

jquery.description: CDN URL for jQuery (required by DataTables)

component.description: Component name where this table lives

target.description: CSS selector for the table element (e.g., '#employees', '.data-table')

data.description: State path for table data array (e.g., 'state.employees', 'store:users.list')

title.description: Column header text (optional, uses 'data' if omitted)

render.description: Custom render function as code string. Receives (data, type, row, meta). Return HTML string.

component.description: Render a UAM component in this column (alternative to render function)

props.description: Props mapping. Values are property paths from row data (e.g., { 'itemName': 'name' })

reactive.description: Auto-update table when data changes (preserves sort/page state)

order.description: Preserve sort order on data update

page.description: Preserve current page on data update

search.description: Preserve search filter on data update

preserveState.description: What table state to preserve during reactive updates

stateProperty.description: Component state property to store DataTable instance (for method access)

deferInit.description: Initialization timing. 'afterPaint': defer to requestAnimationFrame (library needs rendered dimensions). 'afterMount': defer to next microtask (DOM must settle). 'immediate': init synchronously.

scanAfterRender.description: This integration creates DOM that may contain framework components. Frameworks requiring explicit scanning (WildflowerJS) should scan after render.

flatpickrIntegration.description: Flatpickr date/time picker configuration. Compilers generate framework-appropriate input binding, picker initialization, value synchronization, and cleanup.

component.description: Component name where this picker lives

target.description: CSS selector for the input element (e.g., '.date-input', '#startDate')

value.description: State path for the selected date value (e.g., 'state.selectedDate'). Two-way bound.

stateProperty.description: Component state property to store Flatpickr instance (for method access)

deferInit.description: Initialization timing. 'afterPaint': defer to requestAnimationFrame (library needs rendered dimensions). 'afterMount': defer to next microtask (DOM must settle). 'immediate': init synchronously.

scanAfterRender.description: This integration creates DOM that may contain framework components. Frameworks requiring explicit scanning (WildflowerJS) should scan after render.

selector.description: CSS selector for the sortable container(s)

handle.description: CSS selector for drag handle within items

itemKey.description: Data attribute name for item identifier (e.g., 'card-id' for data-card-id)

containerKey.description: Data attribute name for container identifier (e.g., 'col-id' for data-col-id)

onSort.description: Called when item is sorted within same list

onMove.description: Called when item is moved to different list

onEnd.description: Called when drag ends (sort or move)

deferInit.description: Initialization timing. 'afterPaint': defer to requestAnimationFrame (library needs rendered dimensions). 'afterMount': defer to next microtask (DOM must settle). 'immediate': init synchronously.

scanAfterRender.description: This integration creates DOM that may contain framework components. Frameworks requiring explicit scanning (WildflowerJS) should scan after render.

cdn.description: CDN URL for SortableJS (auto-included if not in scripts)

store.description: Store name to call method on

params.description: Parameters to extract from event and pass to method

genericIntegration.description: Generic third-party library integration for libraries not explicitly supported

cdn.description: CDN URL to load the library

global.description: Global variable name the library exposes (e.g., 'Sortable', 'Chart')

init.description: Initialization code to run after library loads. Has access to the global variable.

mountHook.description: Code to run when component mounts. Useful for per-component library setup.

unmountHook.description: Code to run when component unmounts. Useful for cleanup.

instances.description: Named instances of this integration within components

deferInit.description: Initialization timing. 'afterPaint': defer to requestAnimationFrame (library needs rendered dimensions). 'afterMount': defer to next microtask (DOM must settle). 'immediate': init synchronously.

scanAfterRender.description: This integration creates DOM that may contain framework components. Frameworks requiring explicit scanning (WildflowerJS) should scan after render.

pluginReference.description: Reference to a UAM plugin from /www/js/uam/plugins/

oneOf.description: Plugin name (shorthand, uses default variant resolution)

variant.description: Force a specific variant (e.g., 'universal', 'wildflower', 'vue'). Default: auto-resolve based on target framework.

config.description: Configuration options passed to the plugin at initialization

global.description: Override the global variable name for this plugin

mode.description: hash: Uses URL hash (/#/path) - works with static hosting. history: Uses History API (/path) - requires server-side routing fallback.

type.description: view: Routes map to view state values. component: Routes render different components.

stateBinding.description: For view-based routing: store.property path that controls the view (e.g., 'ui.view')

viewTransitions.description: Enable View Transitions API for smooth page transitions

transitionClass.description: CSS class applied during view transitions

path.description: URL path pattern. Use :param for dynamic segments (e.g., '/product/:id')

name.description: Unique route name for programmatic navigation

view.description: For view-based routing: the view state value this route represents

component.description: For component-based routing: component name to render

params.description: Map URL params to state paths (e.g., { 'id': 'ui.selectedProductId' })

queryParams.description: Query parameters to sync with store state (e.g., ['category', 'search'])

queryParamBindings.description: Map query params to specific state paths (e.g., { 'category': 'ui.categoryFilter' })

transition.description: Per-route transition control: false to disable, true to use default, or string for custom transition name

before.description: Guard code run before entering route. Return false to cancel, or a path string to redirect. Has access to: from, to, params, query.

after.description: Code run after route loads successfully. Has access to: from, to, params, query.

guards.description: Route guards for authentication, authorization, or data loading

bindingSource.description: Explicit binding source - eliminates ambiguity about data origin

oneOf.description: Standard source types: state, props, computed, item, index

source.description: state: Component state. props: Component props. computed: Computed property. item: Current loop item. index: Current loop index (0-based).

path.description: Property path (e.g., 'count', 'user.name', 'items.length'). For 'index' source, path is optional.

oneOf.description: Store binding - access global store data
Expression binding - for complex JavaScript expressions

expression.description: JavaScript expression to evaluate. Has access to: state, props, computed, item, index, helpers, stores.

oneOf.description: Helper call binding - call a helper function with arguments

helper.description: Helper function name from the helpers section

args.description: Arguments to pass to the helper, each can be any binding source

styleBinding.description: Style binding supporting both static and computed styles

oneOf.description: Single binding source that returns a style string or object
Object with individual style properties, each can be static or bound

strategy.description: visibility: CSS display toggle (element stays in DOM, preserves state). presence: DOM insertion/removal (element removed, state lost).

modelBinding.description: Two-way data binding for form elements

path.description: State path to bind (must be writable - typically state source)

type.description: Element type - determines event (input vs change) and value extraction (value vs checked)

provides.description: Context to pass to handler. 'value' extracts event.target.value automatically.

args.description: Additional arguments to pass, each resolved from a binding source

class.description: CSS class binding - should return class string

style.description: Inline style binding - can be binding source, or object with per-property bindings

transition.description: CSS transition for element enter/leave. Applied when element is conditionally shown/hidden.

loopNode.description: List rendering. Provides context: item (aliased by 'as'), index (0-based number), count (total), first (boolean), last (boolean). Supports nested loops - inner loops can access outer loop context via their 'as' aliases.

as.description: Item variable name for template access

key.description: Unique key path for efficient reconciliation (e.g., 'id' resolves to item.id)

template.description: Template for each item - can contain nested loops

empty.description: Template to render when array is empty

filter.description: Optional filter expression. Has access to item and index. Return true to include.

trackBy.description: Property path for identity tracking during updates (alternative to key)

transition.description: Transition for list item add/remove animations.

enter.description: CSS class for item enter animation

leave.description: CSS class for item leave animation

codeDefinition.description: Code definition for computed properties and methods with optional dependency and generation hints

uses.description: Store names this code accesses. Compilers inject store references via getStore().

usesHelpers.description: Helper function names this code accesses. Compilers make helpers available.

expensive.description: Hint for computed properties: whether this derivation is expensive (array filter/reduce/sort). When true, AI generators should use memoization (useMemo, createMemo, Pinia getter). When false, inline access is fine.

depends.description: Explicit dependency list for memoization (e.g., ['products.all', 'ui.categoryFilter']). Helps AI generators produce correct dependency arrays.

params.description: Named parameters for methods (e.g., ['product']). AI generators should use these instead of generic 'args' objects.

modifiesState.description: Hint for methods: whether this method mutates store state. Helps AI generators decide whether to wrap in set() (Zustand) or use produce() (Solid).

navigates.description: Hint for methods: whether this method involves route navigation. Helps AI generators inject router/navigate dependencies.

value.description: Rule parameter: number for min/max/length, string/preset for pattern, field name for match, code for custom

rules.description: Validation rules (checked in order, first failure stops)

static.description: Hint: data never changes at runtime. AI generators should use a plain const instead of a reactive store.

init.description: Initialization code (runs when store is created)

persistence.description: Persistence configuration for automatic state saving/loading

storage.description: Storage type: 'local' for localStorage, 'session' for sessionStorage

autoSave.description: Automatically save state on every change

debounce.description: Debounce save operations by N milliseconds

watch.description: Watch handlers for reactive state changes. Equivalent: React useEffect with deps, Vue watch, Solid createEffect, WF watch block.

destroy.description: Cleanup code when store is torn down. For clearing intervals, closing connections, unsubscribing.

subscribe.description: Declarative subscriptions to other stores. Enables cross-store reactivity. Note: subscribe schema may be refined in v8.

additionalProperties.description: Array of state paths to observe (e.g., ['cpuLoad', 'items'])

onStoreUpdate.description: Called when a subscribed store path changes. Receives (storeName, path, newValue, oldValue).

refs.description: Non-reactive instance references. For third-party library instances (charts, maps, sortable), DOM references, and values that persist without triggering reactivity.

subscribe.description: Store subscriptions - auto-injects stores via this.stores and triggers onStoreUpdate lifecycle hook

additionalProperties.description: Array of state paths to subscribe to (e.g., ['items', 'selectedId'])

integrations.description: Component-level integration instances. References integration types defined at manifest level.

additionalProperties.description: Integration instance name (must match an instance defined in manifest integrations)

beforeInit.description: Called before DOM bindings. Use to access this.listItem for components in lists.

mount.description: Called after component mounts to DOM

onStoreUpdate.description: Called when subscribed store paths change. Receives (storeName, changes) arguments.

watch.description: Watch handlers for reactive property changes.

refs.description: Non-reactive instance references. For third-party library instances (charts, maps, sortable), DOM references, and values that persist without triggering reactivity.

onError.description: Error handler code. Receives (error, errorInfo). Return false to stop propagation.

fallback.description: Template to render when an error occurs.

templateNode.description: A node in the component template tree

events.description: Event handlers (key is event name: click, input, etc.)

loopTemplateNode.description: Standalone loop node (alternative to element.loop for cleaner nested loops)

bindingNode.description: Inline text binding (for mixed static + dynamic text)

props.description: Props to pass - values can be literals or binding sources

slot.description: Default slot content to render inside component
